title: Task outcome: Read-only audit focused on installer/new PC/GitHub recovery/version parity/hook approval for Din
summary: Completed read-only audit of DinoBrain installer/new PC/GitHub recovery/version parity/hook approval surfaces. Found one P0 risk: update/reinstall can strand unpushed local commits by force-resetting existing app/data branches with git checkout -B origin/ref. Found P1 gaps: Windows GUI blocks documented no-Git ZIP fallback; release/version parity can drift because package version, tag, setup metadata, and installer refs are not enforced together; uninstall removes Claude MCP but leaves the installed Claude UserPromptSubmit hook. Hook approval design itself preserves manual trust and has verification coverage.

request: Read-only audit focused on installer/new PC/GitHub recovery/version parity/hook approval for DinoBrain. Inspect installer/setup/update/reinstall/uninstall docs/release/verification scripts, return P0/P1 findings with line evidence, fixes, and consensus candidate statements; no edits.
decisions: Do not edit repository files during this audit.
Treat existing dinobrain-data local commits as protected user data; installer should stop rather than reset them.
Treat Codex hook trust as a manual security boundary; approval helper may guide but must not bypass trust.
next_steps: Patch Checkout-DinoBrainRef/Sync-DinoBrainRepo to use ff-only update semantics and fail on local-ahead/diverged branches, especially for dinobrain-data.
Allow the Windows GUI to run the PowerShell no-Git ZIP fallback or update docs/UI to make Git a hard prerequisite.
Add release/version gates that require package version, installer metadata, tag, TargetCommitish, and published asset refs to agree before replacing a release asset.
Add Claude settings cleanup to uninstall plus regression coverage.

Hook approval design itself preserves manual trust and has verification coverage.
